programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers faulty bulletproof vests in an abandoned truck filled with stolen USMC merchandise, and attempts to locate the source of the cargo and any vests that could have been sent to military personnel overseas. Crime drama, guest starring Challen Cates, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS

Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In their first case without trusty colleague Jack, the team members reinvestigate the disappearance of PE teacher Jason Bowe when the remains of a body are discovered near the elite public boarding school where he taught. Unfortunately, their snooping could not have come at a worse time for the staff, who are preparing to welcome a local MP to open their new computer centre - but what they do glean tells them the dead man seemed very much a loner. However, it soon turns out he got on much better with his students, forming close and inappropriate relationships with several. But could his behaviour have rattled someone enough to kill him? Susannah Harker and Nicola Walker guest star

programme.desc: Wesley discovers an old racing car, which Compo, Clegg and Foggy offer to help him transport back to his shed, not reckoning on its condition. Long-running rural comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde, with Gordon Wharmby

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wally is called upon to settle an argument between Compo and Foggy - the exact size of Nora Batty's feet. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and Joe Gladwin star

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: DCI Adams' investigations into a spate of domestic robberies result in an accusation of sexual harassment against him from a young nurse. Hetty is convinced he has been wrongly accused and investigates the claim from her hospital bed

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: A drug dealer dies while in police custody and Sam suspects a cover-up within the unit. He orders a full investigation, though it seems none of the other officers is prepared to help. To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra reopens an investigation into the suspected suicide of a Foreign Office diplomat, whose body was found in a frozen London lake after she had suffered the personal tragedy of a miscarriage and the professional embarrassment of having a government laptop stolen from her home. The woman's fiance and twin sister both believe her death is connected to sensitive information held on the missing computer and blame her boss for her downfall - and when Sandra clashes with the man himself, it only makes her more determined to pursue the conspiracy theory, despite Strickland's advice to tread carefully. Simon Day and Sharon Small guest star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Old adversary Linda Cummings taunts Boyd from inside a secure psychiatric hospital, leading the team to discover more murder victims within its confines. As his frustrations rise, Boyd needs help from Grace - but she has been mysteriously forced to take time away from the Cold Case Squad and handed the investigation over to an eminent psychologist who is an expert on Cummings. Guest starring Gina McKee, Ruth Gemmell and Alexander Siddiq

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart is horrified to discover his trusted driver and friend is the mystery drug dealer the 'Postman', who plans to use him as his inside man at Sun Hill, using incriminating photos and unexplained payments into his bank account to blackmail him. Jo rumbles what is going on, but Stuart's efforts to put matters right just lead to greater danger. Sid Owen guest stars

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A woman's corpse is discovered in a ploughed field, and the only lead is her husband, a potter. A year earlier, he suddenly decided to start a new life as a monk, suggesting that his wife may have committed suicide because she could not bear to lose him to the brethren - or that her spouse may have had a hand in her death. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

It features the Iris Prize Film Festival as well as sessions and masterclasses. The prize offers the winner £1,000 as well as a unique and personal mentoring session

programme.title: Fflam
programme.desc: Noni continues to enjoy the stranger's company and chooses to spend more time with him rather than home on the farm with Deniz. The question remains however, is 'Tim' playing a dangerous game or playing with Noni's emotions? Drama, starring Gwyneth Keyworth and Richard Harrington

programme.title: Noson Lawen
programme.desc: Mair Tomos Ifans presents an evening of entertainment with artists from Meirionnydd. With Gai Toms, Gwibdaith Hen Frân, Sion Goronwy, Sian Meinir, Lois Wyn and Mared Jeffery

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a family affair when two brothers buy a bungalow for their mother in Blurton in Staffordshire and elsewhere property developers buy a huge barn in Upwell in Norfolk and a large three bedroom flat in the south London district of Tooting. Presented by Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a woman who had her life savings stolen by a man she met online and fell in love with

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special episode looking into knife crime in the UK. In London, a Metropolitan police officer is the victim of a vicious knife attack

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help teams look for items at an antiques fair in Detling, Kent, while Charlie Ross visits Maidstone Museum to find out about photographer Christina Broom

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A young nurse, Luca, battles to help his patients whilst dealing with ghosts from the past. Medical drama, starring Ross McLaren and Melanie Kilburn

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets a couple of creative Californians keen to make a lifestyle leap to the Welsh borders, and also discovers how llamas are thriving in the British countryside

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades constructs a bird bath from old crockery, and the Flog It! team share their tips on buying animal-related antiques

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on items including a vintage train set, a set of mid-century chairs, a 1940s style microphone, and an old butchers block

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes Lexi from Hereford to the bidding room, who arrives with a miniature toy steam engine handed down from father to daughter. Next, Frank from Rochdale brings in an antique cash register he rescued from a tip and lovingly restored, and Steve hopes to impress the dealers with a series of plywood boards from London's famous Marquee club, which have been signed by an array of seminal rock bands

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Helle and David travel from Wiltshire with a diamond and sapphire engagement ring passed down three generations of marriages, seeking the help of goldsmith Richard Talman. Next up, Luthier Julyan Wallis restores Jimmy Spencer's Hofner electric guitar, before metal expert Dominic Chinea works on a 200-year-old church weathervane. Finally, leather expert Susie Fletcher turns her attention to an old fly-fishing bait wallet

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: Rose Matafeo: Horndog
programme.desc: Rose performs her acclaimed stand-up show, a whirlwind of mid-twenties angst and fizzing obsession recorded in London's West End. The set enjoyed sell-out performances around the world before being crowned Best Show at the Edinburgh Comedy Awards in 2018

programme.title: Caitlyn Jenner: Honesty
programme.desc: The world's most famous transgender woman Caitlyn Jenner explains why honesty is the key to peace of mind. In 1976, Caitlyn - then known as Bruce - won Decathlon gold at the Montreal Olympics. She was called the world's greatest athlete. Fame and adulation followed but gender dysphoria - where there's a mismatch between someone's biological sex and their gender identity - was always bubbling away under the surface, until 2015, when Caitlyn finally came out as a transgender woman

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman, ex-Great Britain swimmer Fran Halsall and Olympic 4x400m bronze medallist Anyika Onuora join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste hear from a man who started an online relationship with a woman in France, whose incessant pleas for money turned to horrifying threats

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Investigating the surge in coughing attacks on police officers, as the Covid-19 pandemic brings fresh dangers for emergency service workers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen and Rob are devastated as the consequences for Tom, Ella and Simon become evident. Ruhma tries to help parents-to-be when the reality of their situation is revealed

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A carpet magnate is killed in an accident at his warehouse, but his youngest daughter suspects foul play and hires Frank and Luella to investigate her own two sisters

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Dorset to help a couple with £475,000 find a rural property that fits the bill when it comes to a workshop and providing space to keep hens. Jules also delves in the county's glove-making heritage and produces his own bespoke pair

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Nick Bailey optimises a narrow garden space, the Flog It! team shares secrets on how to nab a bargain at a car boot sale, and a dowdy kitchen gets a new lease of life

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin tells Ruby he has booked a trip at Easter, while Kat is sulking over Phil but spots Stas and an idea forms. Keegan worries that the rent money has been spent on a night out, but Tiff reckons she can sort it with Jack. Isaac is upset that Lola isn't returning his calls, so Patrick offers his son some advice

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: In the semi-final, the last four competitors face a breed that judge Colin Taylor is world-famous for grooming - the lamb-like Bedlington terrier. In Dogology, crossbreed puppies enter the salon and the groomers must identify the breeds of their two parents. One of the most dramatic transformations takes place on the Dogwalk when the dogs take the shape of completely different animals, to the delight of their owners. Presented by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a Victorian terrace in York, a plot of land in Rainham, Kent and a flood-damaged Cumbrian property

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. The story takes a bizarre twist as the mysterious woman known as Maryanne starts making financial demands of them too

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent Police officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands Police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams find potentially profitable items at the East of England showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp attempts to get to the bottom of an ancient mystery

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A simple missing person's case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre where it's clear that no one is what they seem. Simon Williams and Josie Lawrence guest star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King head off into the heart of traditional Japan to find out about the historic roots of the nation's cuisine. Dave puts his Strictly fancy footwork into action by kneading noodle dough the artisan way, before the duo then take an awe-inspiring journey to see Mount Fuji, spend a night at a Buddhist temple and are entertained by one of Kyoto's geisha complete with traditional drinking games

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a property hunting mission in Oxfordshire with a young couple who are both medical professionals and have a sizable £1.5million budget to find a country home for their growing family. Away from potential properties, Jonnie braves the Oxfordshire waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades makes a useful magnetic tool organiser, while Dom Chinea creates a planter complete with decorative house numbers. For the big build the pair vie to make the most coveted of household features - the kitchen island. From the archive, Rachel de Thame digs up an unwanted garden tree and Gay Search reveals a clever way to turn an unappealing side return into an attractive outdoor space

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Alice, from Wales, tries to sell a moulded model advertisement for men's underwear from the early 20th century. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Sharon from Hellsby tries to interest the dealers in a Victorian silver belt. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former world champion boxer Anthony Crolla, Commonwealth silver medallist high jumper Morgan Lake, former British tennis number one Greg Rusedski and Olympic medal-winning swimmer Steve Parry. Sue Barker hosts

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: After a day spent house-hunting, the newlyweds take up an invitation from Nessa and Smithy to relax with a trip to the bowling alley. However, when tensions between Gavin and Stacey reach a head at the end of the night, their two friends begin to regret making the offer

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey is fuming that Lily has ordered glam holiday stuff on her credit card and vows to make Ruby pay for it. Dotty pitches her idea to a distracted Ruby, who eventually agrees to allow Dotty and Tiff to host tables. Lola is fed up about Isaac, Kim offers to match Mila next, and Tiff and Keegan celebrate their new room at Sonia's

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Kate Winslet, who talks about her role in romantic Victorian drama Ammonite. Accomplished actor Stanley Tucci discusses his role as a writer suffering dementia in Supernova, Pirates of the Caribbean's Orlando Bloom chats about powerful revenge drama Retaliation, while rising British star Kingsley Ben-Adir talks about his part in sci-fi anthology Soulmates. Plus, Silk City featuring Ellie Goulding perform New Love

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Cameras eavesdrop on more tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions. Geordie Shore star Nathan Henry is desperate to know if ex Tommy was only with him for the fame. Tallia Storm finally reveals the real reason she dumped racing driver Seb Morris six years ago. Will they be able to clear the air and try again, or will the truth leave their exes in exile?

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: The series rounds off with former Apprentice candidate Ryan-Mark Parsons finding out why he was fired from his relationship by Oskars, while actor and broadcaster Mim Shaikh is served up some shocking home truths by his ex, Destiny

programme.title: Tropic Thunder (2008)
programme.desc: A director's epic Vietnam movie is behind schedule and over budget. Taking the advice of the grizzled war veteran on whose life the film is based, he drops the actors into a jungle with the intention of filming them fighting guerrilla-style - but the cast is unaware the surrounding warfare is genuine. Comedy, directed by and starring Ben Stiller, with Jack Black, Steve Coogan and, in an Oscar-nominated role, Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The globetrotting presenter looks back on some of the most incredible wildlife stories he has encountered, from a giant tortoise sanctuary in the Seychelles to armed conservationists defending a forest in Belize. Catching up with old friends, Simon discovers how one of his programmes help protect a crucial whale sanctuary off the coast of Australia

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright is joined by actor and presenter Barney Walsh.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Mr Blandings Builds His Dream House (1948)
programme.desc: A city couple set out to build a home in the wilds of rural Connecticut. They discover they have bitten off more than they can chew thanks to a host of legal problems and an apparently endless stream of cowboy workmen and high-priced materials. Comedy, starring Cary Grant, Myrna Loy, Melvyn Douglas and Reginald Denny

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley visit Villeurbanne in Lyon, France, for their next potentially lucrative venture, where Kate buys a pair of vintage ice skates

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to marine invertebrates - bizarre and beautiful animals that thrive in the toughest parts of the ocean. Divers swim into a shoal of predatory Humboldt squid emerging from the depths to hunt in packs, and timelapse photography reveals starfish gathering under the Arctic ice to devour a seal carcass. A camera also follows a female octopus into a cave, where she hides to protect her eggs until she starves to death

programme.title: Sailing
programme.desc: The final race of the Prada Cup stage of the America's Cup, as Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli face Ineos Team UK for a spot in the 36th Cup match.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Claire Rawle and James Lewis are at St Albans Cathedral, where items to be valued include a whalebone walking stick and an autograph book with signatures of the Rolling Stones

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Plans are made to improve the mental health of the meerkats and wolverines, a koala has a nasty eye infection, and the flamingos show off their unusual nest building skills

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the farmers begin their preparations for the colder months ahead. Father and Daughter team, Frank and Georgia Hunter continue to find ways to adapt and survive on their 600-acre mixed livestock farm. Meanwhile in Northumberland, Ewan and Emma decide to find a new, bigger farm, and in the Lake District, Matt's vintage temperamental bailer keeps breaking down as he attempts to bring in the hay

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the third and final programme, Trump sets his sights on Asia, and determined to go ahead with his new tough approach, he launches the biggest trade war in economic history. Trump's unorthodox style is on full display as he switches between maximum pressure and maximum flattery, as he forms the most unlikely of friendships with one of the world's most brutal dictators, Kim Jong-un

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up Jamali Maddix comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by Fern Brady and Sindhu Vee

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A man is murdered for no apparent reason by a guest at a party. Detectives use CCTV and financial transactions to track the suspect and arrest him just before he flees the country. The forensic team employ a mixture of traditional and digital forensics to try to determine whether or not the witnesses are telling the truth

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The story of Princess Latifa, who was thwarted in an attempt to escape Dubai in 2018, with newly released video footage from her secret phone that shares her account with the world

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Michelle Ogundehin splits the contestants into three teams, each of which is given an open-plan office space to transform for a technology client, a sustainable beauty brand and a media team. The brief is to create a work space, a break-out space and a meeting space within the office, and Michelle will be looking for both displays of signature style and work that suits the needs of the client

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a woman who had her life savings stolen by a man she met online and fell in love with

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades constructs a bird bath from old crockery, and the Flog It! team share their tips on buying animal-related antiques

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as the second group of quarter-finalists give some scruffy bichons frisés their trademark white cloud look, and try to identify a cross-breed's two parents. In the final challenge, they must trim their dogs' coats to create a homage to a place close to their hearts, before showing off their creations on the Dogwalk. But who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor take through to next week?

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates how to improve flexibility and balance

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as Terry and Simon from Essex take on Trace and Mark from Lancashire, competing to build the most valuable collection of antiques for a chance to win £1,000

programme.title: The Truth About Lions
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, Big Cat Diary presenter Jonathan Scott investigates the behaviour of a pride of lions in East Africa as he discovers what makes these animals tick. He explores the possible reasons for the lions' social structure, including the benefits of pack-hunting and raising cubs, and reveals the findings of Professor Craig Packer's 30-year study of the creatures

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley search for bargains at an antiques fair in Surrey, where the items that catch their eyes are a pair of vintage angel wings and an apple ladder

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The strategies adopted by plants that enable them to grow in every possible environment, even where there is no sunlight, water or nutrients. Featuring the dragon's blood tree, which survives in the desert by converting moisture found in the mist and shading its own roots, the carnivorous Venus flytrap that has evolved into an extraordinary predator able to digest insects, and the cat's claw creeper, which uses other plants as a ladder to reach sunlight. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers group is glad when Rocket Dog has her second litter of pups to replenish their dwindling numbers

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Disaster strikes at the Whiskers' burrow, and the Commandos are on the warpath when Zorro disappears

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southern are in Margam Country Park near Port Talbot to value items brought in by the public. Paul Martin visits St Fagans National History Museum

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: In a special programme to end this series, Mary is in conversation with British writer Jed Mercurio. He's the mastermind behind hit show Line of Duty, returning for a new series in the spring, and has a rich back catalogue of attention-grabbing TV drama including Bodyguard, Cardiac Arrest and Bodies. How much does his early medical and military training inform his writing? And how much drama does it take to make real events into unmissable television?

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson cooks more warming, comforting and inspirational recipes, beginning with a supper perfect for winding down at the end of a long day - with the menu featuring chicken with red grapes and Marsala, followed by a sunken chocolate amaretto cake with crumbled amaretti cream. She also prepares her take on mussels with pasta, as well as her autumnal radicchio, chestnut and blue cheese salad, and an egg tortilla pie featuring a crisp crust, soft-yolked eggs, salty ham and melted cheese

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Suzanne and Andrew have a huge problem in their lives - a giant derelict swimming pool in their back garden in Plymouth. A succession of landscaping companies have run a mile the moment they gaze into the abyss, and their budget does not even stretch to simply filling it in - so how will designers Tom Massey and Pip Probert transform this unique outdoor space? Meanwhile, Joel Bird comes to the help of an Essex couple with a nightmare patio. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dr Chris Van Tulleken is joined by his brother Xand and Dr Guddi Singh to talk through the latest developments and answer current concerns. Though the effect of the coronavirus pandemic has been devastating to many, the team reveal the breakthroughs in genetics, medicine and modelling that have provided a way out of this situation and given hope and confidence that, in the event of a future pandemic, it can be taken on and beaten

programme.title: Top of the Pops: The Story of 1979
programme.desc: A look back at a heady year for the chart show, which recorded its highest audience of 19 million viewers, as physical format singles peaked at four times that number. Social problems in Britain during the Winter of Discontent did not stop inventive musicians from making hay as eager fans queued up to embrace their output, and new wave, ska, reggae, rock, folk and electro hits competed for the top slots. Among the featured artists are Gary Numan, Nile Rodgers, Jah Wobble and Woody from Madness

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the third and final programme, Trump sets his sights on Asia, and determined to go ahead with his new tough approach, he launches the biggest trade war in economic history. Trump's unorthodox style is on full display as he switches between maximum pressure and maximum flattery, as he forms the most unlikely of friendships with one of the world's most brutal dictators, Kim Jong-un

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Investigators examine the home of a man covered in blood who hands himself into the police declaring he has murdered his wife, but who also claims to have no memory of the incident. Crime scene coordinator Jo Ward carries out analysis of the property in the hope of piecing together a sequence of events, but a crack in the man's mobile provides an insight into his motives

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry challenges the celebrity cooks to prepare the perfect breakfast for houseguests. Two of the contestants adopt a Mexican theme, pitting quesadillas against breakfast burritos, while another presents the judges with an edible crossword. Mary and fellow judges Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin each select their favourite dish, but even this is no protection against being eliminated from the contest. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining seven celebrity home cooks are put through their paces once more. First, Mary Berry challenges the contestants to make their ultimate burger and chips, before Chris Bavin reveals frozen peas as the secret ingredient that the celebrities must make the star of the dish for his rustle up challenge on day two. The three judges must then decide who will be cooking again in this week's eliminator challenge, following one of Angela's toughest recipes to date

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John Baptiste hear from a man who started an online relationship with a woman in France, whose incessant pleas for money turned to horrifying threats

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Nick Bailey optimises a narrow garden space, the Flog It! team shares secrets on how to nab a bargain at a car boot sale, and a dowdy kitchen gets a new lease of life

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Peter Mason from Herefordshire arrives with an old-fashioned ice-cream maker that belonged to his beloved Granny Ethel, and holds memories of happy times at her house churning vanilla ice cream together. Meanwhile, organ restorer David Burville takes on a Spanish barrel organ, known as an organillo. Later, the experts restore a portable 1950s record player and a leather document case

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: On Feel Good Friday, Mark Wright revisits his favourite exercises from the week

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as James and Simon take on Kim and Scott, going head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Tanya for a chance to win £1,000

programme.title: The Truth About Lions
programme.desc: Big Cat Diary presenter Jonathan Scott and Professor Craig Packer conclude their investigation into the creatures' social habits. They examine the use of the mane, the role of murderous stepfathers in the lion's family life, and the challenges the animals face for survival at a time when their presence has become restricted to small parts of Africa

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: With £1,000 to spend and half their items going to auction, it remains to be seen whether Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawlsy will be able to turn a profit at all. Kate banks on a pair of designer shoes and Caroline puts her faith in a 17th-century chest. It's a tense race to the finish as they discover the week's overall winner

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to apes and monkeys, revealing their similarities to humans. Large armies of hamadryas baboons battle on the plains of Ethiopia to steal females and settle old scores, while Japanese macaques beat the cold by lounging in thermal springs. An orang-utan baby fails in its struggle to make an umbrella out of leaves, and capuchins try to smash nuts with a large rock. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk is joined by chef Anna Haugh in the historic kitchens of one of the UK's finest stately homes to cook food enjoyed at some of the royal family's favourite haunts - dishes they have enjoyed with family and friends, at picnics and in restaurants. Anna creates a meat pudding that would have found favour with Bertie, Prince of Wales, and his mistress Lillie Langtry and cooks up a mille feuilles dessert from the recipe book of a Buckingham palace kitchen maid, hidden in the Royal Archive for over 100 years. Plus, historian Dr Polly Russell visits the restaurants given the royal seal of approval in the 1950s and '60s

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show's travels around the country, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset, Sandon Hall in Staffordshire and Powderham Castle in Devon. The antiques experts include Catherine Southon, Adam Partridge, Philip Serrell, Mark Stacey, Claire Rawle and David Fletcher, with items ranging from some folk art Scandinavian nutcrackers to a Napoleonic prisoner of war handcrafted box. Presenter Paul Martin also takes a look around the stately Firle Place in East Sussex

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games for the final time, prior to the week's overall winner being declared

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: It is training time for barn owls Rowan and Cedar, recently arrived from Colchester Zoo, while senior keeper Erika patiently attempts to win the confidence of Sumatran tigress Dharma. At Highland Wildlife Park, a member of the public enjoys the company of Arktos and Walker the male polar bears when she takes part in a Keeper for the Day experience

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at some of the floral highlights of 2020. It is a celebration of some of the star flowers of gardens, from tulips and roses to salvias and dahlias. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their own gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The presenter treks through the southern fjords of Norway to find the stunning Oseberg ship. This lost 1,000-year-old vessel transformed the fierce reputation of the Vikings and became a symbol of a nation's fight for freedom

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Pioneer of the ever-growing contemporary London jazz scene, Moses Boyd started playing drums aged 13. His affinity for a wide range of music, from grime and electronica, to the gospel, soul and funk he heard growing up in south London have heavily influenced his unique sound. He joins the host to discusses his musical journey, loves and influences, illustrated by classic clips from the show's archives. Jools also catches up with legendary American folk musician Peggy Seeger, who performs All in the Mind, and Rag'n'Bone Man, who gives a first performance of his new single All You Ever Wanted

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Empire of the Sun (1987)
programme.desc: An English boy living in Shanghai is separated from his parents during the Japanese invasion and forced to live on the streets. The youngster is befriended by an American sailor, who helps the boy adapt to life without his family, before the pair are eventually captured and subjected to the terrors of internment. Steven Spielberg's Second World War drama based on JG Ballard's semi-autobiographical novel, starring Christian Bale, John Malkovich and Nigel Havers

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: While on the algae planet Tyrol discovers the Temple of Five, and the Cylons take an interest in the find as it may lead to a return to Earth. Athena learns her baby Hera may still be alive, and Lee requires assistance from Anders. Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber star

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Admiral Adama faces a life-changing decision when he has to decide whether to keep the Eye of Jupiter, which could ultimately lead mankind to Earth, or destroy it to prevent the Cylons from finding it. Edward James Olmos, Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber star

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. The first edition looks back over Trump's first year and a half in office, when he fell out spectacularly with some of America's oldest allies, and offers an inside look at crucial meetings and summits where the US President shocked his counterparts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into the Bolton steeplejack's love of restoring engines and chimneys, highlighting one of his greatest triumphs - the steam traction engine he painstakingly repaired over 27 years. His great appreciation for volunteers who dedicated their lives to preserving the past is also explored

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a fascinating scene of the sun dropping below the horizon

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb explores the hidden dangers of properties built in the Tudor period by looking at the new style of multi-room homes that only the aristocracy had previously been able to afford, which were now being constructed by the emerging wealthy classes. Plus, the presenter looks at the exotic new goods brought in from the New World by merchants that could actually be deadly

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: The new Queen finds herself pressured by Parliament to marry and produce an heir. Second part of the award-winning period drama chronicling the life of Elizabeth I, starring Glenda Jackson, Ronald Hines and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Fireworks for a Tudor Queen
programme.desc: The historian teams up with materials scientist Zoe Laughlin in an ambitious attempt to recreate one of the earliest and most spectacular firework displays in Britain. The extravagant festivities were originally laid on by Robert Dudley, a close friend of Queen Elizabeth I, as an attempt win the Virgin Queen's hand in marriage in 1575, and Lucy travels to Italy for clues on how to make the Tudor equivalent of a Catherine Wheel

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: After finding a broken surfboard on his local beach, Jhonny Guerrero, a teenager from one of Peru's toughest barrios, sets his heart on becoming a professional surfer. This documentary follows Jhonny as surf champion Sofia Mulanovich takes him under her wing and gives him a chance to succeed in the sport

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a fascinating scene of the sun dropping below the horizon

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into the Bolton steeplejack's love of restoring engines and chimneys, highlighting one of his greatest triumphs - the steam traction engine he painstakingly repaired over 27 years. His great appreciation for volunteers who dedicated their lives to preserving the past is also explored

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb explores the hidden dangers of properties built in the Tudor period by looking at the new style of multi-room homes that only the aristocracy had previously been able to afford, which were now being constructed by the emerging wealthy classes. Plus, the presenter looks at the exotic new goods brought in from the New World by merchants that could actually be deadly

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the strong bonds between Great Britain and Denmark, starting at the very tip of Jutland where the North Sea meets the Baltic. Nicholas Crane investigates how after defeats in the Napoleonic wars and the loss of lucrative farming land the Danish put poor soil to work rearing pork, and made a big business out of exporting bacon. Plus, a retired man takes on the tough challenge of the North Sea Beach Marathon

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into the steeplejack's love of steam-powered engines and machinery. After spending a large part of his life studying their construction and history, he passed on this knowledge in his programmes and highlighted the importance of steam power in Britain's past

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cabin beside a stream standing at the foot of a mountain, contrasting the majesty of nature with the innocence of the tiny dwelling

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James realises he has a rival for Helen's affections, while Siegfried reaches breaking point with Miss Harbottle. Tristan has another all-nighter with the bell ringers, leading Siegfried to believe he has too much time on his hands - so he puts him in charge of some newly acquired piglets

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters

programme.title: In Conversation
programme.desc: The actress discusses her life and career in an interview recorded at the British Film Institute Southbank, from her movie debut in Educating Rita through roles in Billy Elliot, Calendar Girls and Paddington. The first in a series of events celebrating leading figures in British cinema. Presented by Richard E Grant, who cast Walters in his semi-autobiographical directorial debut Wah-Wah

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank's journey takes him to Australia, where he examines Aboriginal rock art, visits St James's Church in Sydney and travels through crocodile-infested waters. In Indonesia, he attends a traditional funeral involving an animal sacrifice, before visiting the Tau Tau effigies of Torajaland. The final stop on this stage of the trek is Cambodia and the ruined city of Angkor Wat

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a cabin beside a stream standing at the foot of a mountain, contrasting the majesty of nature with the innocence of the tiny dwelling

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into the steeplejack's love of steam-powered engines and machinery. After spending a large part of his life studying their construction and history, he passed on this knowledge in his programmes and highlighted the importance of steam power in Britain's past

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Fireworks for a Tudor Queen
programme.desc: The historian teams up with materials scientist Zoe Laughlin in an ambitious attempt to recreate one of the earliest and most spectacular firework displays in Britain. The extravagant festivities were originally laid on by Robert Dudley, a close friend of Queen Elizabeth I, as an attempt win the Virgin Queen's hand in marriage in 1575, and Lucy travels to Italy for clues on how to make the Tudor equivalent of a Catherine Wheel

programme.title: Katherine Jenkins: From Wales to the World
programme.desc: Since Neath girl Katherine Jenkins burst onto the music in 2003 she has had 13 number one albums, making her one of the world's best-selling classical singers. In a documentary originally shown to celebrate her 40th birthday in June 2020, the mezzo-soprano looks back over her career highlights, talks about hitting the big screen in her first feature film and reveals how she has kept busy during the lockdown, with weekly online performances for her fans and a special VE Day concert in an empty Royal Albert Hall. The programme also includes a tribute from Katherine to the NHS and key workers

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on September 6, with performances by artists including Adamski, Mariah Carey, Talk Talk, the Farm, Deee-Lite, Bombalurina, Caron Wheeler, Janet Jackson and Loose Ends

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on September 13, with performances by artists including Londonbeat, Sonia, Janet Jackson, INXS, Bass-O-Matic, Maria McKee, the KLF, Steve Miller Band and Faith No More

programme.title: St David's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A rummage through the BBC's music archives, featuring a selection of tracks from some of the most important and innovative Welsh artists of the past few decades. The programme includes performances by Manic Street Preachers, Catatonia, Super Furry Animals, Marina and the Diamonds, Stereophonics, Feeder, Shakin' Stevens, Bonnie Tyler, Shirley Bassey and Tom Jones

programme.title: Tom Jones' 1950s: The Decade That Made Me
programme.desc: Tom Jones fronts the first of four retrospective documentaries in which celebrated musicians look back at the decades that defined them. For Tom, that decade is the 1950s, the era following the austerity of the Second World War that saw a boom in popular culture, which swept aside the old order and ushered in a new era of entertainment. The veteran singer provides a first-hand guide to his formative years in a small mining community in south Wales, revealing how he - much like many people of his generation - turned to TV, movies, radio and music to find a voice for himself. Revisiting Treforest and Pontypridd, where he spent his childhood and teenage years, Tom recalls his joy when rationing finally ended, his encounter with American GIs stationed near his home, and the arrival of rock 'n' roll and the 'Teddy Boys'. Includes contributions by writers Joan Bakewell, Katherine Whitehorn and Michele Hanson, and historians Alwyn Turner, Dr Martin Johnes, Tony Russell and Francis Beckett

programme.title: Dame Shirley Bassey: Electric Proms 2009
programme.desc: Trevor Nelson and Edith Bowman present highlights of the singer's performance to celebrate her 50 years in showbusiness. Classic tracks such as Big Spender and Goldfinger appear alongside songs from her album The Performance, with accompaniment by the BBC Concert Orchestra and guest collaborators David Arnold, James Dean Bradfield and Richard Hawley

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Introduced by Fearne Cotton, the popular and successful Welsh rockers perform an intimate concert of tracks from their 2019 UK number one album Kind, alongside songs from their much-loved back catalogue including Dakota, Have a Nice Day and Mr Writer

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: DJ Trevor Nelson and singer-songwriter Corinne Bailey Rae present their ultimate soundtrack in an hour of classic and contemporary soul and R'n'B gems. Given an airing are childhood favourites such as the Jackson 5 and Gladys Knight. as well as inspirational tracks from Prince, Mary J Blige, Stevie Wonder and Marvin Gaye. As they watch their selection, the duo reveal the reasons behind their choices

programme.title: Tom Jones' 1950s: The Decade That Made Me

When they arrive, the local Jewish community are in full swing getting ready to celebrate the holiday festival of Purim. This is where we finally get to learn the full story behind Purim and why it's so important to the Jewish community. Then comes Mishloach Manot and Sophia gets to give out special Purim gift bags to friends, family and their local elderly community. programme.title: My First
programme.desc: We follow Sophia and her family as they celebrate the Jewish holiday of Purim. After travelling across to the local kosher bakery, Sophia and her family are shown the traditional food Hamantaschen, before having a go at trying to make their own! Delicious! Once they finish up, it's time for her and her family to put together their own costumes ready to attend the synagogue. When they arrive, the local Jewish community are in full swing getting ready to celebrate the holiday festival of Purim. After decorating the traditional noise-makers, Sophia is ready to use it during the reading of the Megillah. This is where we finally get to learn the full story behind Purim and why it's so important to the Jewish community. Then comes Mishloach Manot and Sophia gets to give out special Purim gift bags to friends, family and their local elderly community. programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. After a tropical rain during the night at Safari World, Andy has been making sure all the animals are safe. Apart from being a little damp, thankfully they’re all okay. Mr Hammond calls Andy and Jen worried that slugs might be eating the giant lettuce he is growing specially for the Galapagos tortoises. Jen and Andy check the cameras and thankfully the slugs have not got to the lettuce – well, not yet at any rate. Jen knows that putting broken shells on the soil around plants deters slugs and snails from eating them. She then discovers on the park’s database that a fish called a tusk fish that has a collection of broken shells. The only problem is, there are no tusk fish at Safari World. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. Jen checks the park database, which has lots of information about African elephants, but it doesn’t say at what age they learn to swim. programme.title: Caitlyn Jenner: Honesty
programme.desc: The world's most famous transgender woman Caitlyn Jenner explains why honesty is the key to peace of mind. In 1976, Caitlyn - then known as Bruce - won Decathlon gold at the Montreal Olympics. She was called the world's greatest athlete. Fame and adulation followed but gender dysphoria - where there's a mismatch between someone's biological sex and their gender identity - was always bubbling away under the surface, until 2015, when Caitlyn finally came out as a transgender woman

programme.title: Caitlyn Jenner: Honesty
programme.desc: The world's most famous transgender woman Caitlyn Jenner explains why honesty is the key to peace of mind. In 1976, Caitlyn - then known as Bruce - won Decathlon gold at the Montreal Olympics. She was called the world's greatest athlete. Fame and adulation followed but gender dysphoria - where there's a mismatch between someone's biological sex and their gender identity - was always bubbling away under the surface, until 2015, when Caitlyn finally came out as a transgender woman

Isaac is upset that Lola isn't returning his calls, so Patrick offers his son some advice

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: In the semi-final, the last four competitors face a breed that judge Colin Taylor is world-famous for grooming - the lamb-like Bedlington terrier. In Dogology, crossbreed puppies enter the salon and the groomers must identify the breeds of their two parents. One of the most dramatic transformations takes place on the Dogwalk when the dogs take the shape of completely different animals, to the delight of their owners. Presented by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a Victorian terrace in York, a plot of land in Rainham, Kent and a flood-damaged Cumbrian property

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. The story takes a bizarre twist as the mysterious woman known as Maryanne starts making financial demands of them too

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent Police officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands Police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams find potentially profitable items at the East of England showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp attempts to get to the bottom of an ancient mystery

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A simple missing person's case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre where it's clear that no one is what they seem. Simon Williams and Josie Lawrence guest star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King head off into the heart of traditional Japan to find out about the historic roots of the nation's cuisine. Dave puts his Strictly fancy footwork into action by kneading noodle dough the artisan way, before the duo then take an awe-inspiring journey to see Mount Fuji, spend a night at a Buddhist temple and are entertained by one of Kyoto's geisha complete with traditional drinking games

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a property hunting mission in Oxfordshire with a young couple who are both medical professionals and have a sizable £1.5million budget to find a country home for their growing family. Away from potential properties, Jonnie braves the Oxfordshire waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades makes a useful magnetic tool organiser, while Dom Chinea creates a planter complete with decorative house numbers. For the big build the pair vie to make the most coveted of household features - the kitchen island. From the archive, Rachel de Thame digs up an unwanted garden tree and Gay Search reveals a clever way to turn an unappealing side return into an attractive outdoor space

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Alice, from Wales, tries to sell a moulded model advertisement for men's underwear from the early 20th century. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Sharon from Hellsby tries to interest the dealers in a Victorian silver belt. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former world champion boxer Anthony Crolla, Commonwealth silver medallist high jumper Morgan Lake, former British tennis number one Greg Rusedski and Olympic medal-winning swimmer Steve Parry. Sue Barker hosts

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: After a day spent house-hunting, the newlyweds take up an invitation from Nessa and Smithy to relax with a trip to the bowling alley. However, when tensions between Gavin and Stacey reach a head at the end of the night, their two friends begin to regret making the offer

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey is fuming that Lily has ordered glam holiday stuff on her credit card and vows to make Ruby pay for it. Dotty pitches her idea to a distracted Ruby, who eventually agrees to allow Dotty and Tiff to host tables. Lola is fed up about Isaac, Kim offers to match Mila next, and Tiff and Keegan celebrate their new room at Sonia's

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Paddy Raff Show
programme.desc: More sketches featuring the popular stand-up comedian, showcasing his unique and quirky take on modern life. This episode comprises a compilation of the biggest belly-laughs from the series, the 2020 Christmas special and the original pilot show

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Kate Winslet, who talks about her role in romantic Victorian drama Ammonite. Accomplished actor Stanley Tucci discusses his role as a writer suffering dementia in Supernova, Pirates of the Caribbean's Orlando Bloom chats about powerful revenge drama Retaliation, while rising British star Kingsley Ben-Adir talks about his part in sci-fi anthology Soulmates. Plus, Silk City featuring Ellie Goulding perform New Love

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Cameras eavesdrop on more tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions. Geordie Shore star Nathan Henry is desperate to know if ex Tommy was only with him for the fame. Tallia Storm finally reveals the real reason she dumped racing driver Seb Morris six years ago. Will they be able to clear the air and try again, or will the truth leave their exes in exile?

programme.title: Tropic Thunder (2008)
programme.desc: A director's epic Vietnam movie is behind schedule and over budget. Taking the advice of the grizzled war veteran on whose life the film is based, he drops the actors into a jungle with the intention of filming them fighting guerrilla-style - but the cast is unaware the surrounding warfare is genuine. Comedy, directed by and starring Ben Stiller, with Jack Black, Steve Coogan and, in an Oscar-nominated role, Robert Downey Jr

programme.desc: Tuesday's business in the House of Lords

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Over the last few years, Moroccan migrants who are trying to reach the EU have become YouTube celebrities by blogging about the journey online. They are getting millions of views, but are their tales of adventure glamourising a route that is often really dangerous? BBC's Our World has travelled across Europe to meet these influencers and find out what's behind this latest trend.

programme.desc: As part of BBC Sport's LGBT+ History Month, Clare Balding looks at what it means to be a gay woman in sport. programme.desc: Bing and Sula collect all the toys they no longer play with and sell them in their shop

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: Father-to-be Anton is busy getting the house ready for the new arrival and Mia talks about what it feels like to be a young female in the male-dominated sport of BMX cycling

programme.title: Diary of Britain: Shinty Town
programme.desc: A profile of 15 UK communities in 1978, focusing on one living in the Inverness-shire village of Newtonmore during September, when the A9 bypass was being built. The villagers discuss their hopes and fears for the development, and there is a look at the local shinty team

programme.title: Port

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia is tangled up when her foot gets caught in the Dàineagan's roof after going for a tumble while out skiing, but she is extremely grateful that Biorc is nearby to offer help

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: When they can't find water to irrigate it, Charlie suggests Matilda spits on the seeds. She does, and due to her power, a tuber comes to life when Matilda's saliva touches it

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain
programme.desc: Neen MacKay heads to the far north coast of Scotland, home to some of the country's most remote and least known salmon rivers. From Cape Wrath to John O'Groats, dramatic spate rivers provide anglers with rich pickings
