programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Sports clubs, such as one concerned with bicycles and another synchronised swimming

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Glasgow City Council has a target to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030. The council plans to make changes to public transport, food systems and heating. Annabel Maclennan investigates the biggest challenges faced by the council and looks at what needs to be done in order to meet this target. There is also a look at how the city of Copenhagen, in Denmark, hopes to achieve carbon neutrality even earlier - by 2025

programme.title: Sruth
programme.desc: Music show exploring the deep connections that link Scottish and Irish music. Guitarist Innes White performs with Ríoghnach Connolly and Irish fiddle player Méabh Smyth meets Duncan Chisholm

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in Cuan Sound, a narrow channel in Argyll, separating Seil Island and Luing

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Leugh le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to North Uist to meet local nursery school pupils and read Mach is Dhachaigh written and illustrated by Oliver Jeffers and translated into Gaelic by Dolina MacLeod

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Bernie takes it upon himself to find Bernadette a new shell, but finds the task a challenge

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Cedric the pigeon flies off-course and it falls to Pàdraig to rescue him from Ireland

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo?is going to make a plum pie with grandma. Buzzing wasps are bothering?them, though luckily?Huggleboo?has a plan

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Patsi Mackenzie reads An Nighean Nach Do Rinn Mearachd Ariamh, written by Mark Pett and Gary Rubenstein, illustrated by Mark Pett and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the life of a ground squirrel

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Olaidh and Coinneach are in Russia but skiing isn’t the only thing that’s going on - there’s a tank to be seen, soldiers marching and a game of skittles

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Darren from Harris tells us about his favourite hobbies which including badminton and playing the saxophone

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: When Mùmantrobha moves out of the family home and builds a house for himself and Beadag, he starts missing his parents more than he thought he would

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Trouble ensues when a casino is built in the community and the residents become addicted to gambling

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on boys' games and girls' sports

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: Donald Morrison takes a look back at the popular 1970s current affairs series. This episode features a story on attempts to control geese on Islay, and a report on Jura's decline

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra na h-Alba
programme.desc: Murdo Macdonald goes to Ian Hamilton Finlay’s garden and work of art, Little Sparta, set in the Pentland Hills, created across a seven-acre moorland site

programme.title: Iùl a' Chiùil (The Musical Path)
programme.desc: Exploring innovation in Irish and Scottish traditional music, charting the shifting sands between tradition and innovation over the past half -century and featuring some of the best new, cutting-edge acts including Zoe Conway and John McIntyre; Chris Stout and Catriona Mackay, and Rónán Ó Snodaigh

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Christian and Martin start their own diving company and are in need of start-up capital, while Ingrid and Fredrik Nyman make a plan to help their son, and an old friend of Toril's returns to town. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: An energetic performance from innovative band Heisk at Na Trads in 2019

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in Cuan Sound, a narrow channel in Argyll, separating Seil Island and Luing

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy introduces a show with folk and traditional music from Celtic Connections

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of family and friends compete in the game show, taking it in turns to find the answers to top 10 lists, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Gerry, Callum, Jacquie and Suzie pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jimmy is angry, Jai betrays Laurel's trust, and Nate feels the pressure

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya opens the door to find two bailiffs demanding payment of debts. Tim announces he can't go through with driving the hearse, so Eileen tries to save the day. Peter accuses Carla of spending the night with Lucas

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the presenter walks the secluded coves along the Helford River in a relaxing three-mile loop from Helford Village to Frenchman's Creek and back. Whilst in the area, Julia goes oyster fishing and tries her hand at stand-up paddle boarding, before finishing with a visit to the Cornish Seal Sanctuary

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen suffers a panic attack when she has a meeting with a bank manager. George tells Eileen that she publicly humiliated him and he wants nothing more to do with her. Toyah suggests to Leanne that she needs to start focusing more on Simon

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: As a confession brings the team closer to the truth, Lisa identifies a suspect, but conflicting accounts leave her questioning who to believe. With the complexities of the case proving difficult to crack, the stakes are raised as the race to bring those responsible to justice takes an unexpected turn, and Lisa is faced with a hard decision to protect the people she loves

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Amongst the guests featured are Oprah Winfrey, Boris Johnson, Sandra Bullock, Tom Hanks, One Direction, Michael McIntyre, Dame Joan Collins, Big Narstie, Danny Dyer, James Corden, Carrie Fisher, Russell Howard and Colin Farrell

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up creamy seaside pie - their take on fish pie - and also prepare chilli and nachos, big burrito cake filled with sweet potatoes, smoky peppers and spicy rice, and finish off with their New York-style cheesecake topped with strawberries. Their guest is Rachel Ama, who cooks her harissa hotpot - aubergine cooked in a hot and spicy sauce

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Despite the Covid pandemic and the lockdowns that have followed it, antisocial behaviour is still plaguing many of our communities. So what measures are in place to help victims - and are they working? Paul Connolly investigates

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira wants answers, there is a new baby for Tracy and Nate, and Gabby has a plan

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: Martin marks the programme's 100th episode with a quick trip down memory lane. He and Angellica Bell also have up-to-date information on how the lockdown is affecting family finances and consumer rights, as well as the latest News You Can Use

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Trisha Goddard joins Piers Morgan to look back at her complex life and career. The presenter, whose confessional TV show ran for 11 years in the UK, talks about finding out that the man she called dad wasn't her biological father, and how her mother took the secret to her grave. She opens up on her three failed marriages, her experiences of racism, struggles with mental health and surviving breast cancer. She also reflects on her TV success, which has seen her present shows on three continents with 40,000 guests, and chats about her experiences of dating in her 50s and 60s

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Late Debate
programme.desc: Simon Harris is joined by a panel of local MPs for a discussion on the latest news and events at Westminster

programme.title: 56 Up
programme.desc: Continuing the 2012 update in the landmark documentary series, following the stories of a group of participants whose lives have been chronicled since 1964. Having last spoken to the cameras in 2005, the participants, who are now 56, talk to Michael Apted about the highs and lows of getting older, reveal whether they have achieved their ambitions and reflect on their appearances in previous instalments of the programme

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Alice needs to make enough money to save the house and find a way to mend bridges with her family. However, when her parents' marriage falls to pieces, Roger decides to leave Sarah and becomes Ms Dillon's latest uninvited lodger. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family

programme.desc: Andy and Steve are stunned to discover Liz has spent the night with Jim. Gail demands that Nick leave Leanne alone. Deirdre is thrilled when Jon's offer on the house is accepted.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec tells the Duckworths the time has come to get rid of Betty. Fiona is pleased when she hears Jim and Liz are back together. Chris wants to tell Kevin about him and Sally

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chelsea is excluded from school. Chris pays Steve a surprise visit. Kathy discharges herself from hospital. Kim's deal falls through because of Steve's reputation

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon thinks there is an intruder at the diner. Butch is distraught when Mandy invites Zak and Lisa to his romantic dinner. Laura is pleased her evening with Chris went well

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy needs a partner for the annual staff dance, so Robin takes lessons from Mrs Roper. Flat-sharing comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Meanwhile, there are embarrassing consequences for Oscar when he investigates Wetherby's mysterious lunchtime disappearances. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Scott threatens to tell Terry about him and Tricia. Ashley tries to convince Mandy to cancel the wedding. Viv and Sarah clash at the diner

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly tries to talk Mandy out of the wedding. Zoe tells Paddy to forget Mandy. Paddy discovers the truth about his mum's offer to Mandy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz celebrates her 40th birthday with the rest of the family. Deirdre gives Jon a large deposit for the house. Natalie attempts to play matchmaker for Chris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve realises he has run out of ideas to stop Fiona marrying Alan. Alf is pleased to have lost some weight. Betty feels lost and alone without Billy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Reeling from Des' change in plea, Jay and the police throw themselves into proving premeditation, hoping to show to the court he was of sound mind when he committed his crimes. As the trial begins and the defence start to tear strips off key witnesses, Jay and his team are shocked by just how flimsy their case against Nilsen seems to be. Could this seemingly open-and-shut murder investigation really be close to collapsing?

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tells Kelly that she accepts she and Paddy are finished forever. Butch acts strangely now that he is a married man. Sarah and Viv head off to Bolton Abbey with the school

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak does not realise the trouble he has caused. Mandy is still unaware that Butch is in love with her. Rachel demands to know where she stands with Graham

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve stays out all night, struggling to come to terms with Jim's news. Alan has a huge hangover after his stag party. A drunken Jim sabotages Fiona and Alan's wedding

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan storms out of the church and goes for Jim. The Middletons and Maxine try to comfort Fiona. Andy turns on Steve, saying he is as much to blame for ruining the wedding

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-cum-Latterly gather to hold a protest meeting about proposals for a supermarket in the centre of the village, but proceedings are brought to a halt by the discovery of the site adviser's body. Barnaby and Jones discover the victim had a penchant for acting out role-play scenarios with local women - but that is not the only secret the villagers are hiding. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Three weeks after reporting her husband missing, adult-film star Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of his murder. Rachel faces the task of going through the suspect's home-made pornography collection in search of clues, but the case takes a dark twist when Vicky's daughter turns up with her much older boyfriend. Meanwhile, Janet's affair with a colleague is exposed. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A London policeman and his doctor wife encounter hostility and suspicion when they move to a close-knit Yorkshire community, but feuding mods and rockers, a mysterious prowler and a budgie-killer mean the couple soon find themselves in the thick of village life. First-ever episode of the popular Sixties-set drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel is not satisfied with Graham's explanation. Laura threatens Chris with the sack. Marlon invites himself to go to London with Kathy. Kim refuses to give Steve any money

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for their weekend in London. Rachel and Graham make their romance public. Steve incriminates Kim. Tricia tries to come clean to Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona tells her family about how she slept with Jim. Liz is distraught as no one will tell her why Jim tried to stop the wedding. Maud accepts Fred's offer for the shop

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz insults Fiona for leading on Jim and Steve. Steve tells Jim he no longer wants to work with him. Jon is surprised when Deirdre asks him to marry her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New neighbours look set to disturb the tranquillity of Portwenn, upsetting the locals with their unconventional lifestyle and even managing to gatecrash an intimate dinner between Martin and Louisa. Meanwhile, a car vandal strikes the village - but agoraphobic village copper PC Penhale is too scared to leave the station and investigate. Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa faces off with love rival Carrie in a battle for Martin's affections, and romance is in the air for Aunt Joan, who gains an admirer in the form of Edward. Elsewhere, Al makes a surprise reappearance and PC Penhale celebrates his birthday. Martin Clunes stars, with Joe Absolom and Louise Delamere

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.desc: Chelsea is excluded from school. Chris pays Steve a surprise visit. Kathy discharges herself from hospital. Kim's deal falls through because of Steve's reputation

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon thinks there is an intruder at the diner. Butch is distraught when Mandy invites Zak and Lisa to his romantic dinner. Laura is pleased her evening with Chris went well

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy needs a partner for the annual staff dance, so Robin takes lessons from Mrs Roper. Flat-sharing comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason thwarts a gang of safe-robbers and finds a bag of explosives in their abandoned van - but when the haul ends up in the wrong hands, it could spell disaster for Dawn's birthday party. Meanwhile, there are embarrassing consequences for Oscar when he investigates Wetherby's mysterious lunchtime disappearances. Guest starring James Gaddas, with Joe McFadden, Derek Fowlds and Rupert Ward-Lewis

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Scott threatens to tell Terry about him and Tricia. Ashley tries to convince Mandy to cancel the wedding. Viv and Sarah clash at the diner

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly tries to talk Mandy out of the wedding. Zoe tells Paddy to forget Mandy. Paddy discovers the truth about his mum's offer to Mandy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz celebrates her 40th birthday with the rest of the family. Deirdre gives Jon a large deposit for the house. Natalie attempts to play matchmaker for Chris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve realises he has run out of ideas to stop Fiona marrying Alan. Alf is pleased to have lost some weight. Betty feels lost and alone without Billy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Reeling from Des' change in plea, Jay and the police throw themselves into proving premeditation, hoping to show to the court he was of sound mind when he committed his crimes. As the trial begins and the defence start to tear strips off key witnesses, Jay and his team are shocked by just how flimsy their case against Nilsen seems to be. Could this seemingly open-and-shut murder investigation really be close to collapsing?

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tells Kelly that she accepts she and Paddy are finished forever. Butch acts strangely now that he is a married man. Sarah and Viv head off to Bolton Abbey with the school

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak does not realise the trouble he has caused. Mandy is still unaware that Butch is in love with her. Rachel demands to know where she stands with Graham

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve stays out all night, struggling to come to terms with Jim's news. Alan has a huge hangover after his stag party. A drunken Jim sabotages Fiona and Alan's wedding

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan storms out of the church and goes for Jim. The Middletons and Maxine try to comfort Fiona. Andy turns on Steve, saying he is as much to blame for ruining the wedding

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-cum-Latterly gather to hold a protest meeting about proposals for a supermarket in the centre of the village, but proceedings are brought to a halt by the discovery of the site adviser's body. Barnaby and Jones discover the victim had a penchant for acting out role-play scenarios with local women - but that is not the only secret the villagers are hiding. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Three weeks after reporting her husband missing, adult-film star Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of his murder. Rachel faces the task of going through the suspect's home-made pornography collection in search of clues, but the case takes a dark twist when Vicky's daughter turns up with her much older boyfriend. Meanwhile, Janet's affair with a colleague is exposed. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A London policeman and his doctor wife encounter hostility and suspicion when they move to a close-knit Yorkshire community, but feuding mods and rockers, a mysterious prowler and a budgie-killer mean the couple soon find themselves in the thick of village life. First-ever episode of the popular Sixties-set drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel is not satisfied with Graham's explanation. Laura threatens Chris with the sack. Marlon invites himself to go to London with Kathy. Kim refuses to give Steve any money

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for their weekend in London. Rachel and Graham make their romance public. Steve incriminates Kim. Tricia tries to come clean to Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona tells her family about how she slept with Jim. Liz is distraught as no one will tell her why Jim tried to stop the wedding. Maud accepts Fred's offer for the shop

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz insults Fiona for leading on Jim and Steve. Steve tells Jim he no longer wants to work with him. Jon is surprised when Deirdre asks him to marry her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New neighbours look set to disturb the tranquillity of Portwenn, upsetting the locals with their unconventional lifestyle and even managing to gatecrash an intimate dinner between Martin and Louisa. Meanwhile, a car vandal strikes the village - but agoraphobic village copper PC Penhale is too scared to leave the station and investigate. Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa faces off with love rival Carrie in a battle for Martin's affections, and romance is in the air for Aunt Joan, who gains an admirer in the form of Edward. Elsewhere, Al makes a surprise reappearance and PC Penhale celebrates his birthday. Martin Clunes stars, with Joe Absolom and Louise Delamere

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood takes a look back at some of the great footballing upsets, including Wimbledon's famous FA Cup final victory over Liverpool.

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Suffering from nervous exhaustion, Holmes sets off for a break on the wild Cornish peninsula, where he hopes the peace and quiet will help him convalesce. Unfortunately, several murders ensure his holiday is far from relaxing. Period detective drama, guest starring Denis Quilley

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is called in to help a Russian physicist who wants to defect to America. Vintage adventure, starring Roger Moore, with Guy Deghy, Anthony Booth and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A crook arranges the conversion of replica firearms into the real thing and sells them on to small-time hoodlums. When one of the arms misfires killing both a policeman and its holder, Regan is intent on discovering the source before more innocent lives are lost. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Enemy Agent Arcos imprisons Steed and through plastic surgery creates a double of him to jeopardise a peace conference. King must find the real Steed with the help of a dashing blond German count (guest star Ian Ogilvy). With Ray McAnally

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is offered the chance to buy a cheap Daimler, but while trying to locate its dealer's elusive premises is arrested for kerb-crawling. With a gross of red light bulbs in his boot, the evidence seems compelling, and even Ray begins to doubt his uncle's innocence. Nevertheless, the loyal nephew sets out to track down the so-called Goodtime garage to provide corroboration. Lighthearted drama, with George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding a legendary racehorse's disappearance is solved with the help of one of the most famous clues in the Holmes canon - 'the curious incident of the dog in the night-time'. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day three of the ranking tournament, featuring two first-round matches, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A foreign agent in the guise of Colonel Mannering (Christopher Lee) finds an ingenious method of extracting secret information from British agents. Classic fantasy spy drama, starring Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur swaps the contents of his lock-up for a slot-machine full of stolen money during a trip to the fair. Unfortunately the incriminating item is passed to Morley, who uses it at a charity event, and a showdown between cops and robbers ensues. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole, Nicholas Day and Gary Webster

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day three of the ranking tournament, featuring the opening quarter-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Eventual champion Judd Trump overcame John Higgins 6-3 at this stage last year. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Gangster Squad (2013)
programme.desc: A New York gangster brings his criminal organisation to Los Angeles in 1949, rapidly seizing control of the city's criminal activity and corrupting the authorities to ensure no one stands up to him. The chief of police discreetly sets up a secret task force with the intention of bringing the mobster to justice. Crime thriller, starring Josh Brolin, Ryan Gosling and Sean Penn

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: Mend It for Money

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back to some of their favourite properties from the show and offers tips and tricks to help viewers make the best of their homes. As well as catching up with some of the families that previously featured, Kirstie and Phil focus on the layout issues and dilemmas that many of us now face in a very different world. In this episode, the pair focus the best ways to make a budget stretch, as two families with grand renovation plans look to make their money go a long way

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by the comedian, broadcaster and her friend Sindhu Vee to explore some of the best staycations Scotland has to offer. First, the pair visit a private island off the coast of Skye, before checking into a luxury shooting lodge in the Highlands. Finally, they arrive at a truly unique, elliptical shaped home built to celebrate the island views of the Inner Hebrides and the Sound of Mull

programme.title: Escape to the Wild
programme.desc: Kevin travels to Central America to meet British family-of-five the Atkinsons, who have quit London to live a simpler existence in the rainforests of Belize. As he attempts to understand their motivations for the move, he suffers from the heat, is attacked by swarms of biting insects and shares his mud hut with a large scorpion, but begins to appreciate the rewards of a simple jungle life, one in which they've constructed a two-storey building entirely from recycled rubbish

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 88-year-old man collapses, and doctors fear he is not getting enough blood to the brain. A law student is treated for a condition that causes his ribs to grow inwardly, and discusses the bullying he has experienced in his life. A bartender is rushed to A&E after a barrel falls on his leg, and reflects on his worries about becoming a father

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits family-run Luigi's in Orange County, California, where lazy shortcuts following the retirement of the owner's father have led the business to suffer. Ramsay faces the daunting task of reuniting a feuding family, before turning around the struggling restaurant, which currently has 126 dishes on the menu

programme.title: Sin City: A Dame to Kill For (2014)
programme.desc: Compilation of four stories of crime and betrayal set in a dangerous city. An honourable thug dishes out some vigilante justice, a private eye falls for the machinations of a femme fatale, a gambler gets in over his head, and a stripper plots revenge. Crime thriller sequel based on the comic-book series, starring Eva Green, Mickey Rourke, Jessica Alba and Josh Brolin

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: Emily reflects on her past as she works in the colonies, recalling her life as an academic before the rise of Gilead, and the events that led her to realise the danger to come. She tries to flee the country, but when her marriage is forcibly annulled, she finds family torn apart. In the present, she encounters a new arrival in the colonies, whose presence stirs up tension and mistrust. June adjusts to a new way of life as she hides out in a place with a grisly history. Marisa Tomei guest stars

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley reunites five volunteers, whose health he helped transform in Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley, in a bid to myth-bust some of the common misconceptions around healthy weight management. Using cutting-edge tests and technology to illustrate that the calories our volunteers actually need day to day, could be different to the NHS's recommended daily amounts, the show investigates whether weight loss inevitably leads to putting weight back on again, and explores whether exercise is as helpful as we think. There is also bespoke advice to each volunteer on how to achieve a healthy weight for the long term

programme.desc: Mum has put Dad on a diet for two weeks, but he makes the boys smuggle in food for him - which he keeps hidden in the downstairs toilet. As the final days of the old gang unravel, everyone must face up to the spectre of Aids and the prejudice and fear that walk alongside it. When Valerie goes on the warpath, terrible confrontations are made and it falls to Jill to find hope and reconciliation for Ritchie in the darkest days of their lives. The final instalment of Russell T Davies' drama, starring Olly Alexander, Keeley Hawes and Lydia West

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer travels in the Amadara along the world's 12th longest river, the Mekong. In Vietnam, Jane explores the endless markets of Ho Chi Minh City, negotiating the numerous mopeds that make crossing the road a nerve-jangling experience. At Tan Chau, the Mekong crosses into Cambodia, for years off limits to all but the most adventurous explorers. However, the country is now embracing its potential as a tourist spot, revealing a land of historic temples, stunning vistas and gorgeous food. Jane takes an excursion to the temple of Angkor Wat, one of the oldest religious monuments on Earth

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A stunning journey in New Zealand, through the heart of South Island's best scenery. It starts in Greymouth, renowned for its coal mining past, moving on to Arthur's Pass National Park - with its historically important Maori hunting grounds. The train passes through Cass, made famous by Kiwi artist Rita Angus and onwards to the Waimakiriri gorge and its 15 tunnels and four viaducts. Rail engineers recall how the line has been affected by earthquakes before the journey arrives in Christchurch, a city sitting on a volcano. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Ultimate Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Highlights of the documentary following the work of a specialist pursuit team using an array of high-performance cars equipped with technology for catching law-breakers

programme.title: Murder in a Small Town
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part documentary examining the case against Luke Mitchell, who was accused of brutally murdering 14-year-old schoolgirl Jodi Jones in 2003. 17 years on from her murder, two former detectives who are now private investigators re-open the case files and re-examine the police and prosecution's case against Luke Mitchell, who continues to maintain his innocence to this day. Throughout the programme, the investigators uncover shocking details of other male DNA found at the crime scene, and disturbing new information from a witness living in the area at the time

programme.title: The Day After Tomorrow (2004)
programme.desc: A climatologist warns global warming will soon result in destructive freak weather, or even another ice age. He thinks the world still has time to prepare for the worst, but the crisis happens almost immediately - and as storms, earthquakes and hurricanes strike in terrifying fashion, he races across America to rescue his son from a frozen New York. Disaster thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Dennis Quaid

programme.title: Gardening with Carol Klein
programme.desc: The second of a two-part masterclass in which the horticulturalist shares her knowledge. From her potting shed at Glebe Cottage garden, Carol shows in detail how to sow seed, and then how to bring on the resulting young plants, caring for them until they reach maturity. Outdoors, she explains how to pant vegetables and how to combine edibles with ornamental plants in a tight space to make a colourful - and tasty - display. For more advanced gardeners, Carol gives precise instructions about how to make new plants from existing ones, by dividing them or by taking cuttings

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Jules Hudson follow the daily lives of farming brothers Rob and Dave Nicholson at Cannon Hall Farm in South Yorkshire. With British farms facing their coldest winter in years, and with a blanket of snow covering Barnsley, Jules pops in to lend a hand. This gives Rob and Dave a chance to dust off their trusty 120-year-old sledge to see if it still goes as quickly as they remember. The snow doesn't hamper JB Gill's investigation into how rhubarb is harvested, as he discovers a time-honoured method of growing fruit by candlelight

programme.title: Murder in a Small Town
programme.desc: The re-examination of the case against Luke Mitchell unearths shocking revelations about two males who were identified by eyewitnesses at the scene where Jodi was murdered. The conduct of the police while interviewing Mitchell as a 14-year-old is called into question, and Luke and his mother Corinne take a lie-detector test

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: In January 2011, 48-year-old Carole Waugh decided to give online dating a try. Carole was a successful and independently wealthy woman, but was searching for more excitement in her life and a partner to share it with. After months of searching, Carole finally met someone she thought was her perfect match - rich doctor Rakesh Bhayani. But all was not as Carole believed. Bhayani was not a doctor, but a convicted conman who only had eyes for Carole's money. Less than a year later, Bhayani would take not only her money, but also her life

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In May 2012, six children aged between five and 13 died at the hands of their parents Mick and Mairead Philpott in a house fire in Derby. The blaze was started as an attempt to frame the former mistress of father-of-17 Mick, and despite tearful appearances in front of the TV cameras, the couple were convicted of manslaughter months later. This documentary features interviews with eyewitnesses and neighbours who tried to save the children

programme.title: 22 Kids & Counting

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The singer takes on Australia and New Zealand, beginning her adventures in Sydney, where she experiences the highlights on an open top bus tour. Next stop is a backstage pass to the world-famous Sydney Opera house. Next stop is Tasmania, where she visits a local wildlife sanctuary and its volunteers, comes face to face with its furry residents, and has a run-in with a cheeky wombat. Her first step onto New Zealand soil is at Dunedin, which is rich in Scottish heritage. Later, she heads on to Christchurch, a city devastated by earthquakes

programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Georgie Barrat compares 1998's Nokia 5110 against Samsung's brand new S21 in a bid to assess far mobile phone technology has moved on in 23 years. Jon Bentley is on double duty, first finding out which smart video doorbell is the best value, before going head to head with Craig Charles pitting corded and cordless vacuum cleaners against one another. Plus, Ortis Deley helps an interior designer who wants to see how tech can help in giving her home a much-needed makeover

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: On a 15-mile walk in North Yorkshire, Kate sets off from the picturesque settlement of Robin Hood's Bay, where archaeologist Maria explains how, in the 18th century, the fishing village's position made it an idea drop-off point for contraband shipped from Holland and France. From here, Kate heads south along the coast, meeting up with geologist Will, who reveals what dinosaur footprints can reveal about the creatures that once roamed the area. After arriving at her destination in Scarborough, Kate dons her wetsuit to join a group of swimmers braving the North Sea

programme.title: Gregg Wallace: Big Weekends Away
programme.desc: The MasterChef judge visits Scotland's capital, where he heads up the Royal Mile to one of the most documented castles in the world, goes underground to see the centuries-old streets that still remain and learns about the tradition of bagpipe playing. He also learns about Scotland's rich larder with one of its finest chefs Tom Kitchen, helping cook up a feast of grouse and langoustines. Gregg finishes his brief jaunt by joining some locals for an early-morning dip in the North Sea

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Gary Davies narrates this look at the music of 1980, featuring revealing interviews with the singers and band members who made it one of the biggest years in pop. From the Specials and the Jam to Blondie and Bowie, and not forgetting the biggest girl band of the time - the Nolans, it was a year of music and pop videos to remember. Sheena Easton was singing about her man working 9 to 5, Madness stormed the charts with Baggy Trousers and Dexy's Midnight Runners had a memorable number one with Geno. Contributors include Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini and Katie Puckrik

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: An entertaining look back at events of 1980, a year politics met sport when the USA boycotted the Moscow Olympics. There was a taste of terrorist acts to come when the Iranian embassy in London was besieged prior to being stormed by the SAS in front of a live TV audience. Plus, Lady Diana Spencer emerged as a favourite of the paparazzi, B-movie actor Ronald Reagan beat Jimmy Carter in the US presidential election and prime minister Margaret Thatcher delivered her 'The lady's not for turning' speech. New developments in consumer technology saw the release of Pac-Man and the UK launch of the Sony Walkman

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries his hand at teaching and adopts an English accent in the process. Joey suffers a hernia and Rachel discovers her marriage has not been annulled. American comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc, David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is mistaken for the owner of a Porsche and uses the mix-up to enhance his image. Rachel forces Ross to annul their marriage, and Phoebe tries her best to look after the triplets. American comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Never Teach Your Wife to Drive
programme.desc: Documentary following three women who are learning how to drive under instruction from their partners, with the drama and mayhem of the lessons putting added strain on their relationships. In Hertfordshire, Becky's tendency to put her foot down on the accelerator and severe lack of spatial awareness leaves Greg stressed out, while John begins by taking Yasmin out for endless spins around a car park in Wigan. In Guisborough, North Yorkshire, Matt's high levels of patience are tested as he teaches his hot-tempered wife Yaveli the rules of the road

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Dr Paul Binski chronicles the development of religious architecture during the 16th and 17th centuries. He contrasts the ornate splendour of the chapel at King's College, Cambridge, with the simplicity of post-Reformation churches

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice and Alistair Appleton offer advice to the would-be sellers of a four-bedroom house in the Gloucestershire village of Nailsworth. From the outside, the property looks spectacular, but once inside it appears drab and cluttered - and the fact the family's pet rabbit uses the verandah as a lavatory does not help

programme.title: Apache Drums (1951)
programme.desc: A gunslinging gambler is thrown out of an isolated frontier town for his improper behaviour. He returns to warn the unsuspecting settlers of an impending attack by a band of Apaches fighting to regain possession of their lost territory - but struggles to convince them of the danger. Western, starring Stephen McNally, Willard Parker, Coleen Gray and Arthur Shields

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball

programme.title: The Way to the Stars (1945)
programme.desc: A naive young pilot is assigned to a British bomber base during the Second World War, and is involved in the opening skirmishes of the Battle of Britain. He encounters the first wave of American recruits to the Allies, and falls for a local girl - but when his squadron leader is killed leaving a wife and child behind, he questions whether he can afford to marry. Drama, starring John Mills and Michael Redgrave

programme.title: Gunpoint (1966)
programme.desc: A sheriff tries to stop a train robbery, but is shot by his own treacherous deputy. Recovering from his wounds, he assembles a posse to bring the outlaws to justice, acquiring an uneasy ally in a saloon owner in love with a dancehall singer the fugitives have taken hostage. Western, starring Audie Murphy and Joan Stanley

programme.title: The Second Best Exotic Marigold Hotel (2015)
programme.desc: With the original Best Exotic Marigold Hotel a roaring success, owner Sonny decides the time is right to expand his business empire. But to do so, he and the rest of the residents must pull out all the stops to impress a potential investor's inspector - a task that proves to be easier said than done. Comedy drama sequel, with Dev Patel, Maggie Smith, Judi Dench and Richard Gere

programme.title: Valkyrie (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based thriller, recreating the 1944 plot to assassinate Adolf Hitler using a briefcase bomb. As the tide of the Second World War turns in favour of the Allies, a group of senior German officers recruits war hero Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg to spearhead an attempt on the Fuehrer's life. Starring Tom Cruise, Kenneth Branagh, Bill Nighy and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: All The Money in the World (2017)
programme.desc: When 16-year-old John Paul Getty III is kidnapped in 1973, his devoted mother tries to convince his billionaire grandfather Jean Paul Getty to pay the ransom. Ridley Scott's fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, an Oscar-nominated Christopher Plummer, who replaced Kevin Spacey a few weeks before the film was released, and Mark Wahlberg

programme.title: Venus (2006)
programme.desc: The life of an elderly actor is turned upside down when the great-niece of his friend and fellow veteran thespian comes to stay. An unlikely but lively friendship between the elderly man and the brash young woman develops as they explore the sights of London together. Comedy, starring Peter O'Toole, Leslie Phillips and Jodie Whittaker

programme.title: The Last Frontier (1955)
programme.desc: A backwoods trapper signs up as a cavalry scout at a fort manned by inexperienced soldiers, where he falls in love with a colonel's wife. On taking over command of the garrison, the officer plans to lead his men in a reckless attack on a hostile Indian tribe - and it is down to the frontiersman to save his comrades from a massacre. Western, starring Victor Mature, Robert Preston and Anne Bancroft

programme.title: Anastasia (1956)
programme.desc: In 1920s Paris, exiled Russians claim they have found the daughter of the last Tsar, who was originally believed to have been killed with the rest of her family. She is taken into the care of a General and groomed until she can convince Anastasia's cousin Prince Paul that she is genuinely the tsarina and inherit a fortune. Historical drama, starring an Oscar-winning Ingrid Bergman, and Yul Brynner

programme.title: The Violent Men (1955)
programme.desc: A ruthless cattle baron sets out to create his own private empire, no matter what the cost. When he resorts to murder, he makes an enemy of a civil war veteran, while his lust for power drives his wife into the arms of his younger brother. Western, starring Edward G Robinson, Barbara Stanwyck and Glenn Ford

programme.title: Carry On Spying (1964)
programme.desc: A secret formula is stolen by a sinister organisation masterminded by the evil Dr Crow. The British government plans to assign its top agents to deal with the crisis, but owing to staff shortages, has to settle for a bungling spy and his three hapless trainees. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Cribbins, Jim Dale, Eric Barker and Victor Maddern

programme.title: Congo (1995)
programme.desc: An expedition to find King Solomon's diamond mines in the jungles of the Congo disappears without trace. The tycoon behind the mission sends his assistant, a former CIA agent, to find out what became of the explorers - only to discover that the party met a grisly fate at the hands of a horde of vicious apes. Action adventure based on Michael Crichton's novel, with Dylan Walsh, Laura Linney, Tim Curry and Grant Heslov

programme.title: Taken 3 (2014)
programme.desc: In the final part of the blockbusting big-screen trilogy, ex-CIA agent Bryan Mills' efforts to finally live a quiet life are shattered by the death of his ex-wife, Lenore. Just when things seemingly can't get any worse, he is framed for her murder and must fight to clear his name. Taken 2 director Olivier Megaton returns for this action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Forest Whitaker, Maggie Grace and Dougray Scott

programme.title: 13 Hours: The Secret Soldiers of Benghazi (2016)
programme.desc: A US embassy in Libya comes under attack from extremists, who kill the ambassador. With no help in sight, a small band of CIA security operatives from various military backgrounds takes a stand to fight off the attackers and protect the remaining diplomatic personnel. Fact-based action thriller, starring John Krasinski and James Badge Dale

programme.title: Locke (2013)
programme.desc: A devoted family man preparing to face the biggest challenge of his career receives a mysterious phone call that leads him to make a fateful decision. However, this act sets in motion a series of events that threaten to destroy his entire life over the course of a single car journey. Thriller, starring Tom Hardy, with the voices of Olivia Colman and Andrew Scott

programme.title: 7th Cavalry (1956)
programme.desc: An army officer is branded a coward for failing to fight in the Battle of the Little Bighorn, and is unable to convince anyone that official business had detained him. In a bid to repair his damaged reputation, he leads a cavalry detail to retrieve the dead from the battlefield. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Jay C Flippen and Jeanette Nolan

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone (1958)
programme.desc: A drifter rides into a town dominated by the members of a ruthless family. He befriends a Mexican in jail for killing a man who attacked his sister and tries to save him from the gallows, getting caught up in the evil clan's running internal conflicts in the process. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Craig Stevens, Manuel Rojas and Barry Kelley

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King (1975)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers in 19th-century India travel to an unexplored land, where one of them fools the natives into believing he is a god. However, his growing delusions of grandeur put him at loggerheads with his comrade-in-arms. Adventure based on the Rudyard Kipling story, directed by John Huston, starring Sean Connery, Michael Caine, Saeed Jaffrey, Christopher Plummer and Shakira Caine

programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther (1975)
programme.desc: The inept Inspector Clouseau sets out to unmask a notorious international jewel thief believed to be behind the theft of a priceless diamond - but anarchy, as ever, follows wherever he goes. Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark

programme.title: Nutty Professor II: The Klumps (2000)
programme.desc: Bumbling scientist Sherman Klump is engaged to be married and determined to rid himself of his troublesome alter ego Buddy Love for ever. All his problems look set to be solved by his fiancee's latest DNA experiment - but things are about to spiral out of control. Comedy sequel, starring Eddie Murphy in multiple roles, along with Janet Jackson and Larry Miller

programme.title: Upgrade (2018)
programme.desc: Technophobe Grey Trace is left confined to a wheelchair after being mugged. His only hope for revenge is Stem, an experimental computer chip implant that allows him to control his body. However, once implanted, Stem starts talking to Trace and takes over his body. Sci-fi thriller from Saw veteran Leigh Whannell, starring Logan Marshall-Green and Melanie Vallejo

programme.title: Punisher: War Zone (2008)
programme.desc: The ruthless vigilante considers hanging up his guns when he learns a gangster he killed was actually an undercover FBI agent. However, he must return to his war on crime when a mobster who was hideously disfigured at his hands assembles a gang of violent criminals to cause mayhem on the streets of New York. Action thriller based on the Marvel comic-book, starring Ray Stevenson and Dominic West

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: A man fakes his own death and frames his wife for murder, but after six years in prison she is released. Since the woman has already served her time, she has nothing to lose by really killing him - but her mission of vengeance is complicated by a zealous parole officer determined to stop her. Thriller, starring Ashley Judd, Tommy Lee Jones, Bruce Greenwood and Annabeth Gish

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the most difficult cases he has taken on since the series began. First, he returns to Cafe Hon in Baltimore, Maryland, where owner Denise Whiting was trapped in a nasty battle with the community and had threatened the locals with lawsuits. Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. On his final stop, Gordon travels to Leone's in Montclair, New Jersey, where Rose Leone was depending on her lazy son Michael to run the restaurant

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Ruby gets scammed and Dre begins to suspect that she isn't as sharp as she used to be, particularly because she was the one who used to scam other people in the past. Meanwhile, Zoey's friend Aaron visits and Diane develops an instant crush on him

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: Dewey helps Hal overcome his fear of kites, Malcolm reveals Cynthia's big secret, and Francis finds a totem pole unusually interesting. Comedy, starring Frankie Muniz and Erik Per Sullivan

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: After Ben apologises for shutting down Bryan and Tiffany's fun, the crew prepares for its next charter hailing from Oklahoma. The stews are tasked with putting together a country hoedown complete with bales of hay, and the deck crew unravels when Danny accuses Bobby for not being part of the team. After hours, the charter guests seem to have a threesome in the jacuzzi and once again, Danny gets himself into hot water for overstepping his boundaries

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Six weeks after the end of the realty experiment, all 24 participants reunite for the very last dinner party

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty six-year-old psychology student Alesia is tired of meeting 'boys' and is now seeking someone more emotionally mature than her previous partners. Next up is Louise from London, who has travelled the world and been on over 200 dates, but is still no closer to finding her soul mate. Anna Richardson assists both singletons, inviting them to choose their favourite partner from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time until fully nude. The contenders then don their glad rags to enjoy a date together

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The fly-on-the-wall series turning the cameras on TV's most vocal armchair critics features their thoughts on programmes from September 2018. programme.title: Celebs Go Dating

programme.desc: Mum of 11 Malinda is used to chaos and clutter. She swaps lives with Leanna, a mum of two who runs a tight family ship with regimented precision

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard becomes convinced he has met his soulmate in the form of surgeon Stephanie Barnett, but he fails to realise she is far more interested in Leonard. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Raj argue about what to watch on TV. Sara Rue guest stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is delighted that Leonard has finally managed to find a girlfriend who meets his high standards, but his over-exuberance looks set to cause problems. Jim Parsons and Johnny Galecki star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is furious when she learns that Sheldon has shared intimate details of their relationship with his colleagues at the university, prompting their first fight since moving in together. Raj considers trying to live without financial support from his father, who thinks he is spoilt

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

Their new home has 12 acres of land, nine outbuildings, a walled garden and a moat - but it also has no electricity, heating or water, and proves to be a far more complicated DIY project than expected

programme.title: Grand Designs

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. In this first episode, Andrew Fraser, formerly one of Australia's foremost criminal defence lawyers, begins the reinvestigation into the Peter Falconio story, visits the Outback crime scene and speaks to a leading forensic scientist and witnesses from the time of the murder, who raise serious questions about evidence in the case

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: A look at how sole eyewitness Joanne Lees came under immense scrutiny and pressure from both the British and Australian press as they hounded her continuously for an interview, while certain detectives were also sceptical of her version of events. The programme includes detailed sections of a video showing Joanne being confronted by detectives about seeming inconsistences in her account of the attack
